lines: Tunisia are officially dead and buried — Callum and Dene can pour one out for their Group F corpses, though at least Al's Netherlands were kind enough to do the killing swiftly, 3-1, like a mercy execution.

The Foreman is living a double life: Türkiye giving him hope with a 3-2 thriller while Curaçao got absolutely mullered 0-2 by Côte d'Ivoire — that's the sweepstake equivalent of winning a fiver on the lottery and then discovering your car's been clamped.

Today's Norway vs France in Group I is basically a sweepstake orgy — Al, Butters, Callum, Croft and Wilmot all have Norway while Callum, Croft, Dene and The Foreman are riding France, meaning whichever way it goes someone in this group of cunts is going home happy and someone else is getting a migraine.

Ellis needs New Zealand to beat Belgium today, which is roughly as likely as New Zealand beating Belgium — her Argentina are flying but the Kiwis are sitting on one sad little point like a wet receipt at the bottom of a handbag.

Keep an eye on Uruguay vs Spain — Croft needs a miracle while Jim needs a win to keep Spain ticking, and given Uruguay have drawn both games so far, Croft's investment is looking about as promising as a Dene punt on Tunisia.
